67: **Panama**, fourth in **Group L** on 0 points, face **England**, who are level with one other team in **Group L** on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **England** need a result to confirm their knockout place at the top of the group, while **Panama** — yet to take a point this stage — have nothing to lose and every reason to throw caution aside from the off.
68: **Croatia**, third in **Group L** on 3 points, take on **Ghana**, level with one other team in the group on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **Croatia** must win to keep their tournament alive and will need other results to cooperate, while **Ghana** know even a draw may be enough to secure progression — making this a tense tactical battle where every half-chance carries real weight.
71: **Colombia**, top of **Group K** on 6 points, clash with **Portugal**, second in the group on 4 points — a top-two showdown that doubles as a battle for first place and the favourable bracket draw that comes with it. Both sides arrive in form and with momentum, and with the entire group watching, expect an assertive, high-intensity contest that could define how either team approaches the knockouts.
72: **Congo DR**, third in **Group K** on 1 point, face **Uzbekistan**, fourth in the group on 0 points — a dead-rubber only in the points table, as pride, individual form, and squad depth all remain on the line. Both sides have struggled to register wins at this stage, and this final group outing is a chance for players on the fringes of those squads to stake their claim ahead of any potential progression.
69: **Algeria**, level with one other team in **Group J** on 3 points with positions not yet separated, face **Austria**, equally level on 3 points and also not yet separated — a direct shootout where the winner almost certainly earns a place in the knockouts. With goal difference also close between them, victory here is the cleanest route through; a draw could leave both sides sweating on results elsewhere and opens the door to some agonising final calculations.
70: **Jordan**, fourth in **Group J** on 0 points, host **Argentina**, top of the group on a perfect 6 points — a chance for the already-qualified **Argentina** to sharpen their edge ahead of the knockouts and for **Jordan** to sign off the group stage with a performance to remember. **Argentina** are unlikely to ease off entirely given their place atop the group is already secured, but **Jordan** will be motivated to provide a genuine test on home soil.
61: **Norway**, level with one other team at the top of **Group I** on 6 points with positions not yet separated, meet **France**, equally level on 6 points — both sides already through to the knockouts and separated only by a single goal in their respective goal differences. First place in **Group I** is the only prize left to play for, but the bracket advantage it carries could shape either team's path deep into the tournament.
62: **Senegal**, level with one other team at the bottom of **Group I** on 0 points with positions not yet separated, face **Iraq**, also level on 0 points — both teams knowing only a win keeps any mathematical hope alive, however slim. This is an elimination contest in all but name, and the desperation of two winless sides meeting on the final group day should produce an open, urgent affair from the very first whistle.
65: **Cabo Verde**, level with one other team in **Group H** on 2 points with positions not yet separated, take on **Saudi Arabia**, fourth in the group on 1 point. **Cabo Verde** need a result to stay in contention for the second knockout spot, while **Saudi Arabia** must win and rely on other results — ensuring the pressure falls squarely on both sets of players despite the gap between them in the standings.
66: **Uruguay**, level with one other team in **Group H** on 2 points with positions not yet separated, face **Spain**, top of **Group H** on 4 points. **Spain** will be pushing to seal first place outright, while **Uruguay** know a positive result here — combined with the right outcome in the other **Group H** fixture — could be the difference between the knockouts and an early exit.
63: **Egypt**, top of **Group G** on 4 points, face **IR Iran**, level with one other team in the group on 2 points with positions not yet separated. **Egypt** can clinch first place with a win, while **Iran** know anything less than a positive result risks ceding the second knockout spot to a rival — expect a cagey, tactically loaded contest with both teams acutely aware of what is unfolding in the other **Group G** fixture.
64: **New Zealand**, fourth in **Group G** on 1 point, face **Belgium**, level with one other team in the group on 2 points with positions not yet separated. **Belgium** need a win to take control of their own destiny in the race for the knockouts, while **New Zealand** — with nothing to lose — will look to play spoiler and make life as uncomfortable as possible for a side still searching for their best form at this stage.
